subject: expose ansible container on nfs mount
commit_message:
expose ansible container on nfs mount

/home/ansible will be exposed in
/dockerdata_fs in order to let
user copy playbook

Change-Id: I01307f461da6e9174127c951c4691b81eddab68b
Issue-ID: APPC-1445
Signed-off-by: Taka Cho <takamune.cho@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.
Removed reviewer Aaron Hay.

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1802/

Patch Set 1:

the get pod result and helm deploy log are on the APPC-1445 comment.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I was not able to deploy this successfully.  This is the error I observed using 'helm deploy --verbose':
 
Error: release onap-appc failed: Deployment.apps "onap-appc-appc-ansible-server" is invalid: spec.template.spec.containers[0].volumeMounts[2].name: Not found: "onap-appc-appc-ansible-server-data"

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

we need to del pvc,pv before do "helm deploy". I have posted the latest log in https://jira.onap.org/browse/APPC-1445

Patch Set 1:

@Takamune I've tried this again is a completely clean cluster and had the same issue.  Is it possible that there is a change/file missing from this review that you have locally?

Patch Set 1:

Hi, @James, I did git pull, reset master/head, then download this gerrit patch in APPC's tenant windriver lab. I posted the steps that I did in https://jira.onap.org/browse/APPC-1445. 

hopefully, that helps

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

I've been unable to reproduce the successful deployment Takamune describes.  Can anyone else verify one way or the other?

Patch Set 1:

This change is for change management use case in R4 where user can upload playbook to ansible container via persistent volume. I have verified worked in Windriver APPC tenant.

Patch Set 1:

i redo again in appc tenant. and updated the log in https://jira.onap.org/browse/APPC-1445

        export NODE_IP=$(kubectl get nodes --namespace onap -o jsonpath="{.items[0].status.addresses[0].address}")
        echo http://$NODE_IP:$NODE_PORT
name: onap-appc
namespace: onap
